message:
fix(templates): distinguish explicit model requests from defaults in override guard

The model-override guard previously checked `vars.Model != ""`, which
was always true since a resolved default model is always populated
before template rendering. This caused false-positive errors when
using legacy (non-template) agent commands with default models.

Changes:
- Add `ModelRequested bool` field to `AgentTemplateVars` to track
  whether the user explicitly requested a model vs. using the default
- Add `templateReferencesModel()` helper that inspects template
  strings for `.Model` or `.ModelAlias` references
- Rewrite the model-override guard to only error when ModelRequested
  is true AND the template does not reference model fields
- Add second guard for template-syntax commands (containing `{{`)
  that still do not reference `.Model` or `.ModelAlias`
- Plumb ModelRequested through spawn, add, restart, and resilience
  monitor code paths
- Update all test agent commands to use template-aware constants
  (testAgentCatCommandTemplate / testAgentBinCatCommandTemplate)
  instead of bare "cat" / "/bin/cat" strings to avoid triggering
  the model-override guard in tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-		Model:       resolvedModel,
-		ModelAlias:  ctx.ModelAlias,
-		SessionName: ctx.SessionName,
-		PaneIndex:   ctx.PaneIndex,
-		AgentType:   agentType,
-		ProjectDir:  ctx.ProjectDir,
+		Model:          resolvedModel,
+		ModelAlias:     ctx.ModelAlias,
+		ModelRequested: strings.TrimSpace(ctx.ModelAlias) != "",
+		SessionName:    ctx.SessionName,
+		PaneIndex:      ctx.PaneIndex,
+		AgentType:      agentType,
+		ProjectDir:     ctx.ProjectDir,
 	})
 	if err != nil {
 		return fmt.Errorf("generating command: %w", err)
